Predecessor genome:
  Mechanism: "EPaxos Revisited re-specifies leaderless consensus by refining dependency tracking, quorum-intersection conditions, and command-ordering rules so replicas can infer conflicts, build a partial order, and use a fast commit path without a fixed leader."
  Niche: "This paper targets low-latency strongly consistent consensus for geo-distributed replication, especially when commands often do not conflict but failures and contention still occur."
  Limitation: "Although it clarifies ambiguities and corner cases, the revisited design still leaves substantial implementation complexity and metadata/dependency-set overhead in recovery and fast-path eligibility checks."

Successor genome (hybridized):
  Mechanism: "FlexRaft is a leader-based consensus protocol that replaces uniform full-replica log storage with phase-aware flexible erasure-coded fragment placement, using coding layouts tailored separately for commit quorums and node recovery so entries can usually be agreed on and repaired without full-object reconstruction."
  Niche: "This paper targets reducing the storage, communication, and recovery costs of fault-tolerant leader-based log consensus while preserving correctness and fast failure repair."
  Limitation: "The design depends on carefully chosen coding parameters and phase-specific layouts, leaving open how to optimally configure and adapt them under changing workloads, failures, and latency conditions in real deployments."

Gene labels in successor:
  G1: mechanism
  G2: niche
  G3: limitation
  G4: observation

For each gene, classify its provenance:
  Predecessor — inherited from the predecessor lineage
  External — brought in from an external lineage
  Novel — newly introduced, not from any known lineage

Also identify the dynamics type.

Key distinction — Mutation requires that the successor's mechanism is clearly derived from (extends/refines) the predecessor's mechanism. If mechanisms are fundamentally different approaches even for a related problem, classify as Speciation.
Hybridization vs Speciation: if the successor's mechanism combines recognizable elements from the predecessor with ideas from another lineage, it is Hybridization, not Speciation.
Speciation vs Niche Competition: Speciation means the successor IS in the same lineage but replaced the mechanism. Niche Competition means no lineage relationship — independent work targeting the same niche.

Answer with one line per field:

Predecessor genome:
  Mechanism: "BiMamba: bidirectional Mamba (state space model) processes sequence in both directions; RC-equivariant architecture ensures predictions are invariant to reverse-complement transformation; trained on human genome."
  Niche: "HyenaDNA is unidirectional (causal); DNA is inherently bidirectional (sense and antisense strands are equivalent); unidirectional models cannot natively capture reverse-complement symmetry of genomic sequences."
  Limitation: "Still limited to DNA; no protein sequence integration; no generative design of functional sequences; cannot model genome-scale (chromosome-level) interactions; variant effect prediction not at the scale of clinical utility."

Successor genome (hybridized):
  Mechanism: "StripedHyena architecture (hybrid Hyena + attention) at 7B scale; trained on 2.7M prokaryotic genomes (1T tokens); single-nucleotide tokenization across entire genome; generative and discriminative tasks unified."
  Niche: "Caduceus models DNA structure but cannot generate functional genetic elements de novo; no model unifies DNA/RNA/protein function prediction and generation at genome scale."
  Limitation: "Trained primarily on prokaryotes; poor generalization to eukaryotic genomes (different chromatin structure, splicing, regulatory complexity); genome-scale generation (chromosome level) still out of reach; no understanding of 3D genome organization."

Speciation vs Niche Competition: Speciation means the successor IS in the same lineage but replaced the mechanism. Niche Competition means no lineage relationship — independent work targeting the same niche.

G1: Predecessor | External | Novel

G4: Predecessor | External | Novel

Predecessor genome:
  Mechanism: "Deep SVDD learns a neural network that maps normal samples into a latent space tightly concentrated around a fixed or robustly estimated center by minimizing hypersphere volume, and scores anomalies by distance from that center under constraints that prevent trivial collapse."
  Niche: "This paper targets one-class anomaly detection, where only normal-class data are available for training and the goal is to detect outliers at test time."
  Limitation: "The method depends on carefully imposed architectural restrictions such as removing biases and controlling activations, which narrows model design flexibility and is needed to avoid degenerate constant solutions."

Successor genome (hybridized):
  Mechanism: "Deep SVDD trains a neural network to map normal samples into latent space and minimize their average squared distance to a fixed center, with a soft-boundary variant that also learns a radius and penalizes points outside the hypersphere while using architecture constraints to prevent collapse."
  Niche: "This paper targets one-class classification and anomaly detection when only target-class examples are available for training."
  Limitation: "The method depends on restrictive architectural constraints and a fixed-center formulation to avoid trivial constant solutions, which limits network design flexibility and may hinder broader applicability."

Gene labels in successor:
  G1: mechanism
  G2: niche
  G3: limitation
  G4: observation

For each gene, classify its provenance:
  Predecessor — inherited from the predecessor lineage
  External — brought in from an external lineage
  Novel — newly introduced, not from any known lineage

Predecessor genome:
  Mechanism: "Generative diffusion over 3D pose space: separate diffusion processes for translation (R³), rotation (SO(3)), and torsion angles; confidence ranking of generated poses."
  Niche: "EquiBind produces single rigid-body pose; real binding is flexible; need multi-modal pose distribution over all degrees of freedom."
  Limitation: "Slow sampling (multiple denoising steps); still requires known protein structure; cannot generate novel ligands; no co-design of protein and ligand."

Successor genome (hybridized):
  Mechanism: "Pairformer replaces Evoformer; diffusion module generates all-atom 3D coordinates for any molecular complex type; trained on PDB structures of all complex types; MSA-free option via single-sequence trunk."
  Niche: "ESMFold (fast, single-chain) and AlphaFold2 (accurate, single-chain) cannot predict complexes; RFDiffusion designs backbones but not full atomic detail with small molecules; no single model covers protein-DNA/RNA/ligand interactions."
  Limitation: "Closed-source weights for commercial use; no generative design capability (prediction only, not design); does not model binding kinetics or conformational ensembles; large gaps in accuracy for RNA-protein complexes."
